summary: Decent early‑90s show with solid front‑row energy; good enough recording (source unknown) that captures highlights like So Many Roads and a nice Brokedown Palace encore.
review: The concert is viewed as an average but enjoyable 1993 performance – fans note a strong front‑row experience, a standout rendition of So Many Roads and a pleasant Brokedown Palace encore, though Jerry’s vocal on Bird Song is criticized. No specific recording flaws are mentioned, suggesting the capture is at least listenable.

ai_rating.rationale: Two moderately positive reviews (4 stars) and one negative review (2 stars) give an average show rating around 3.3; no recording issues are reported, so a neutral‑good recording rating yields an overall weighted score near 3.2.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers do not mention any audio problems, implying the tape is at least clean and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Generally solid but unremarkable; fans describe a decent groove typical of the early‑90s era, with a pleasant encore and no major flow issues.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Front‑row experience praised; vocal on Bird Song criticized, but his singing on So Many Roads highlighted as exceptional.
